message:
fix(core): harden runtime test isolation against TLS destructor panics

The asupersync runtime stores Cx/Sleep state in TLS. When spawned tasks
aren't fully drained before runtime drop, Sleep::drop tries to access
TLS that's already being destroyed, causing an abort.

Wrap runtime.block_on() and runtime drop in separate catch_unwind calls
so that: (1) test assertion panics are captured and re-raised cleanly,
(2) TLS destructor panics during runtime drop are absorbed, and
(3) the process doesn't abort when a test fails under load.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.6 <noreply@anthropic.com>
